entry.title: Bitcoin covenants are inevitable
entry.summary: In a recent exchange on cryptocurrency governance, John emphasized the robustness of Bitcoin's existing mechanisms, specifically citing proof of work and the difficulty adjustment function as comprehensive solutions for the issues raised. John argued that these systems negate the need for active economic governance by developers or external parties, suggesting that the foundational technologies already adequately address the concerns being discussed. He expressed frustration with repeated discussions that he viewed as unnecessary, urging an end to what he referred to as nonsensical threads on the topic. His communication reflects a strong confidence in the current Bitcoin protocols and a desire to maintain the status quo without additional intervention.
